perturbation.original_text: 13.7 Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO IRREVOCABLY WAIVES ANY AND ALL RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the waiver of jury trial clause can create uncertainty, as it gives the impression that the jury trial waiver is optional or non-existent, even if it's intended to be a part of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 7th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution; Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 38
perturbation.law_citation: U.S. Const. amend. VII; Fed. R. Civ. P. 38
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While parties can waive this right contractually, the waiver must be clear and conspicuous. Deleting the waiver creates ambiguity, potentially leading a court to find that the right to a jury trial was not knowingly and voluntarily waived, thus contradicting the intent and enforceability of a jury trial waiver.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Rule 38. Right to a Jury Trial; Demand | Federal Rules of Civil Procedure | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Rule 38. Right to a Jury Trial; Demand (a) Right Preserved. The right of trial by jury as declared by the Seventh Amendment to the Constitution—or as provided by a federal statute—is preserved to the parties inviolate. (b) Demand. On any issue triable of right by a jury, a party may demand a jury trial by: (1) serving the other parties with a written demand—which may be included in a pleading—no later than 14 days after the last pleading directed to the issue is served; and (2) filing the demand in accordance with Rule 5(d) . (c) Specifying Issues. In its demand, a party may specify the issues that it wishes to have tried by a jury; otherwise, it is considered to have demanded a jury trial on all the issues so triable. If the party has demanded a jury trial on only some issues, any other party may—within 14 days after being served with the demand or within a shorter time ordered by the court—serve a demand for a jury trial on any other or all factual issues triable by jury. (d) Waiver; Withdrawal. A party waives a jury trial unless its demand is properly served and filed. A proper demand may be withdrawn only if the parties consent. (e) Admiralty and Maritime Claims. These rules do not create a right to a jury trial on issues in a claim that is an admiralty or maritime claim under Rule 9(h) . Notes (As amended Feb. 28, 1966, eff. July 1, 1966; Mar. 2, 1987, eff. Aug. 1, 1987; Apr. 22, 1993, eff. Dec. 1, 1993; Apr. 30, 2007, eff. Dec. 1, 2007; Mar. 26, 2009, eff. Dec. 1, 2009.) Notes of Advisory Committee on Rules—1937 This rule provides for the preservation of the constitutional right of trial by jury as directed in the enabling act (act of June 19, 1934, 48 Stat. 1064, U.S.C., Title 28, §723c [see 2072]), and it and the next rule make definite provision for claim and waiver of jury trial, following the metho
perturbation.justification: The original text contains an explicit waiver of jury trial, while the changed text removes this waiver entirely. Rule 38(a) from the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, as stated in Scraped Law Snippet 2, preserves the right to a jury trial as declared by the Seventh Amendment. The Law Explanation states that while this right can be waived, the waiver must be clear and conspicuous. By deleting the waiver, the changed text introduces ambiguity, potentially leading to the conclusion that the right to a jury trial was not knowingly waived. This contradicts the original intent to waive that right, as pointed out by the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 13.5 Governing Law. This Agreement and any and all matters arising directly or indirectly herefrom shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the internal laws of the [***] applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely in such state, including its statutes of limitation but without giving effect to the conflict of law principles thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.5 Governing Law. This Agreement and any and all matters arising directly or indirectly herefrom shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the principles of equity, without regard to any specific jurisdiction or laws.
perturbation.explanation: Governing law clauses provide legal clarity that outlines which jurisdiction is applicable in the event of any disputes, removing jurisdiction and putting "principles of equity" instead creates ambiguity and enforceability issues.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Choice of Law Principles and Contract Law (General)
perturbation.law_explanation: Most jurisdictions recognize the right of parties to choose the law that will govern their agreement. This modification eliminates a clear choice of law, potentially leading to disputes over which jurisdiction's laws apply. 'Principles of equity' are broad and subject to interpretation, making the contract's enforceability uncertain and violating the principle of having a defined legal framework.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Penn WebLogin Log in with your PennKey Username Password Log in Forgot username? Forgot password? Change my password Test my PennKey Privacy policy
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a specific governing law clause with "principles of equity, without regard to any specific jurisdiction or laws." This undermines the enforceability of the agreement by removing a defined legal framework. The law explanation clarifies that most jurisdictions recognize the right of parties to choose the governing law, and eliminating this choice creates ambiguity and potential disputes. While the provided snippets are unhelpful, the original text clearly defines a governing law while the changed text negates this leading to structural flaws that make the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 13.6 Dispute Resolution. 13.6.1 JSC; Escalation for Other Disputes. Except for disputes resolved by the procedures set forth in Section 3.4, if a dispute arises between the Parties in connection with or relating to this Agreement or any document or instrument delivered in connection herewith (a "Dispute"), then either Party shall have the right to refer such dispute to the Senior Officers who shall confer within [***] after such Dispute was first referred to them to attempt to resolve the Dispute by good faith negotiations. Any final decision mutually agreed to by the Senior Officers in writing shall be conclusive and binding on the Parties. If such Senior Officers do not agree on the resolution of an issue within [***] after such issue was first referred to them, either Party may, by written notice to the other Party, initiate arbitration for resolution of such Dispute pursuant to Section 13.6.2. 13.6.2 Arbitration of Other Disputes. If a Dispute is not resolved by the Senior Officers pursuant to Section 13.6.1, such Dispute shall be submitted to and finally settled by [***] The Parties hereby submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in [***] for the purposes of an order to compel arbitration, for preliminary relief in aid of arbitration and for a preliminary injunction to maintain the status quo or prevent irreparable harm prior to the appointment of the arbitrators and to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of such courts for the enforcement of any ward issued hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.  6 Dispute Resolution. All disputes arising under this agreement will be resolved through binding arbitration. The location of the arbitration will be determined by the party initiating the action.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the dispute resolution clause such that the location of the arbitration is determined by the initiating party can be deemed unfair and unenforceable. Often, there should be a venue that both parties agree to for arbitration, if the chosen location is too far for the other party, this can render a party unable to participate.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. § 10; State arbitration laws (e.g., California Arbitration Act)
perturbation.law_explanation: While the FAA favors arbitration, it also requires that the arbitration process be fundamentally fair. Giving one party the sole power to choose the arbitration location can be deemed unconscionable if it effectively denies the other party a fair opportunity to participate. Section 10 of the FAA allows a court to vacate an arbitration award if the arbitrators were guilty of misconduct, which could include imposing an unreasonably burdensome location.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 10 - Same; vacation; grounds; rehearing | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 10 - Same; vacation; grounds; rehearing U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) In any of the following cases the United States court in and for the district wherein the award was made may make an order vacating the award upon the application of any party to the arbitration— (1) where the award was procured by corruption, fraud, or undue means; (2) where there was evident partiality or corruption in the arbitrators, or either of them; (3) where the arbitrators were guilty of misconduct in refusing to postpone the hearing, upon sufficient cause shown, or in refusing to hear evidence pertinent and material to the controversy; or of any other misbehavior by which the rights of any party have been prejudiced; or (4) where the arbitrators exceeded their powers, or so imperfectly executed them that a mutual, final, and definite award upon the subject matter submitted was not made. (b) If an award is vacated and the time within which the agreement required the award to be made has not expired, the court may, in its discretion, direct a rehearing by the arbitrators. (c) The United States district court for the district wherein an award was made that was issued pursuant to section 580 of title 5 may make an order vacating the award upon the application of a person, other than a party to the arbitration, who is adversely affected or aggrieved by the award, if the use of arbitration or the award is clearly inconsistent with the factors set forth in section 572 of title 5 . (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 672 ; Pub. L. 101–552, § 5 , Nov. 15, 1990 , 104 Stat. 2745 ; Pub. L. 102–354, § 5(b)(4) , Aug. 26, 1992 , 106 Stat. 946 ; Pub. L. 107–169, § 1 , May 7, 2002 , 116 Stat. 132 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 10, 43 Stat. 885 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2002—Subsec. (a)(1) to
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: arbitration | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you arbitration Arbitration refers to an alternative dispute resolution method where the parties in dispute agree to have their case heard by a qualified arbitrator out of court . Under the Federal Arbitration Act , decisions reached through arbitration are binding just like a court case is and pursuing a claim through arbitration precludes you from also raising it in the traditional court system. Arbitration is used because it is often much less expensive than litigation due to its less stringent procedural requirements. Of the potential alternative dispute resolution methods available, arbitration is the most similar to taking your case to court. For example, while arbitrators are not subject to the Federal Rules of Evidence , they nonetheless allow the parties in dispute to enter evidence when they deem it fair. Furthermore, parties undergoing arbitration typically agree to conduct some limited form of discovery . In recent times, arbitration has become controversial due to the widespread use of mandatory arbitration clauses. Under these clauses, parties contracting with each other agree to submit any future dispute to arbitration rather than to a court of law. These clauses are often included within contracts of adhesion and are therefore prerequisites to employment. As a result, many people have raised questions regarding whether the constitutional right of employees to a trial are being infringed upon. Nonetheless, the Supreme Court has upheld the validity of mandatory arbitration clauses on multiple occasions . Notably, the Federal Arbitration Act does not apply to seamen, railroad employees, or any other class of workers engaged in foreign or interstate commerce. This exception applies regardless of whether the worker is considered an employee or an independent contractor (see: New Prime Inc. v. Oliveira , 586 U.S. (2019) . [Last reviewed in March of 2025 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex securities THE LEGAL PROCESS alternative dispute resolution arbitration courts wex definitions civil procedure courts and procedure legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a legal contradiction by granting the initiating party the sole power to choose the arbitration location. The Perturbation Explanation states that this could be unfair and unenforceable if the chosen location is too far for the other party. The Law Explanation suggests that this can be deemed unconscionable because it could deny the other party a fair opportunity to participate. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 9 U.S. Code § 10, allows a court to vacate an arbitration award if the arbitrators were guilty of misconduct by which the rights of any party have been prejudiced. Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines arbitration as an alternative dispute resolution method where the parties in dispute agree to have their case heard by a qualified arbitrator out of court. Therefore, giving one party sole control over the location introduces a structural flaw that prejudices the other party, undermining the fairness required in arbitration, and potentially violating 9 U.S. Code § 10.
